L1.area: Email engagement tracking and event timeline rendering

L2.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where metadata from tracking requests is stored and later rendered in a timeline view without proper output encoding. An attacker can inject malicious scripts through crafted HTTP request headers sent during tracking interactions.

L3.area: Email tracking pixel open-detail rendering in timeline event views
L3.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where the user agent string captured from email tracking pixel open requests is stored and later displayed in the timeline view showing email open details without proper output escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary scripts by sending a crafted user agent header when triggering the tracking pixel.

ground_truth.reason: The tracking pixel timeline view renders the stored user agent string from email open requests directly into HTML without escaping, allowing cross-site scripting via a crafted user agent header.
